choices: Budgeted value of work actually completed
questions.explanation: EV represents the value of work completed based on the project budget.

choices: Budgeted value of work scheduled to be completed
questions.explanation: PV is the budgeted cost of the work planned to be completed by a specific time.

choices: Actual cost spent on completed work
questions.explanation: AC represents the actual money spent performing project work.

questions.stem: Which value represents the total approved project budget?
questions.explanation: BAC stands for Budget at Completion and represents the original total project budget.

questions.stem: What does a positive Cost Variance indicate?
questions.explanation: A positive cost variance means earned value exceeds actual cost, meaning the project is under budget.

questions.stem: What does a negative Schedule Variance indicate?
questions.explanation: A negative SV indicates less work has been completed than planned.
Cost Variance is calculated as EV minus AC.
Schedule Variance equals EV minus PV.
